title: Turbulent_premixednext_term_combustion_Flamelet_structure_and_its_effect_on_turb
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here is the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ST/SL0 = I0 ∫ -∞ to ∞ Σ dη. (1)
*   ST/SL0 = (AT/AL)(SF,C/SL0) = (AT/AL)I0. (2)
*   I0 = SF,C/SL0, SF,C = ∫ pr OR dξ. (3)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Figure 1: illustrates DNS results of Bell et al. [1,2] and Sankaran et al. [3]. Also shown are LES computations of Pitsch and Duchamp de Lagenest [4].
*   Figure 2: illustrates the areas At and Al of a wrinkled and an unwrinkled flamelet, respectively.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Σ: Flamelet surface area per unit volume.
*   δτ: Brush thickness.
*   I0: Stretch factor.
*   ST: Turbulent burning velocity.
*   SL0: Laminar burning velocity.
*   Aτ: Area of wrinkled flamelet.
*   AL: Area of Unwrinkled flamelet.
*   SF,C: flamelet consumption speed.
*   pr: density of the reactants
*   OR: Instantaneous volumetric reaction rate.
*   3-60 m/s: Laboratory-scale burners operated at mean velocities.
*   0.69-10: Values of u'/SLo varied.
* Reynolds numbers: Associated with laboratory-scale burners
* Burner dimsensions: realistic burner dimensions (from 1 to 5cm)

title: Turbulent_premixednext_term_combustion_Flamelet_structure_and_its_effect_on_turb
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here is the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical equations:**

*   A_T = Integral(Sigma * dV) from -infinity to infinity = Integral(Sigma * A_L * d(eta)) from -infinity to infinity
*   S_T / S_L0 = f( u' / S_L0, l / delta_l0, Ma_T)
*   S_T / S_L0 = f( u' / S_L0, l / delta_l0, Ma_T, W/l, or t/(l/u'))

**2. Graphs/charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   c = 0.5 (surface)
*   AT: area of wrinkled flame surface
*   AL: area of unwrinkled flame surface (= area of c = 0.5 surface)
*   c: reactedness (T-TR)/(TP-TR)
*   D: molecular diffusivity
*   DT: turbulent diffusivity
*   d: eddy diameter
*   I0: stretch factor, Eqs. (1)-(3)
*   K: stretch rate, Eq. (11)
*   Ka: Karlovitz number, Eq. (21)
*   l: streamwise integral scale
*   MaL: Markstein number of a laminar flame Eq. (35)
*   Maf: Markstein number of flamelet in a turbulent flame, Eq. (36)
*   Мат: turbulent Markstein number, Eq. (37)
*   SF.C: local consumption speed of a of a flamelet, Eq. (3)
*   SLO: laminar unstretched burning velocity
*   ST.GC: turbulent burning velocity (global consumption speed), Eq. (15)
*   ST.LC: turbulent burning velocity (local consumption speed), Eq. (16)
*   ST.LD: turbulent burning velocity, (local displacement speed, Eq. (17)
*   u': r.m.s. streamwise velocity fluctuation
*   U: mean streamwise velocity
*   α0: thermal diffusivity of reactants
*   δl0: unstretched laminar flame thickness
*   δT: turbulent brush thickness
*   δPRE: preheat zone average thickness
*   δCH: CH layer average thickness
*   η: coordinate normal to = 0.5 contour
*   ΓK: stretch efficiency factor, Eq. (13)
*   λmin: minimum wrinkle wavelength
*   Σ: flame surface density
*   Σmax: surface density at center of brush
*   ζ: direction normal to the flamelet, Eq. (37)
*   ωR: volumetric mean reaction rate

title: Turbulent_premixednext_term_combustion_Flamelet_structure_and_its_effect_on_turb
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Technical content from the gas turbine research page:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**

    *   Σ = (u'/Sl0)(1-Σ)Cl

2.  **Graphs/Charts:**

    *Figure 3:*
        *Schematic of (a) a hypothetical “geometry-independent” wrinkled flamelet structure
       and (b, c) realistic “geometry-dependent” wrinkled structures that cause the burning                  velocity St to depend on the distance (x) for the Bunsen geometry and the time (t) for the spherical case.*

    *Components and Labels:*
    * a: ST,
    *b: ST, tip,   ST, base
    *c

3.  **Tables:**

    *Table 1:* *Models of premixed turbulent combustion*

    *   *A. Models with no differential equation for Σ*

        1.  BML model with algebraic relation for Σ [7]
        2.  Stochastic particle/Monte Carlo concept [36]
        3.  Fractal model of Lindstedt-Vaos [39]
        4.  Zimont correlation model [37,38]
    *   *B. Flamelet structure approach—differential equations predict Σ*

        1.  Flame surface density balance (CFM/RANS or LES) [18,32]
        2.  G-Equation models (RANS or LES) [4,30]
        3.  Direct numerical simulation [1-3]

4.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

    *   ST (burning velocity)
    *   Io
    *   Σ
    *   δτ
    *   l (local integral scale)
    *   u' (local velocity fluctuations)
    *   Slo
    *   Cl

title: Turbulent_premixednext_term_combustion_Flamelet_structure_and_its_effect_on_turb
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   A2/A1 = exp(Ktres)
*   dΣ/dt + U dΣ/dx + V dΣ/dy = vT d^2Σ/dy^2 + KΣ - M - Q
*   K = -n \cdot (n \cdot V)V + V \cdot V + SL/RC
*   M= (beta0(SL+C sqrt(k))/(YF/YF0)[1-(YF/YF0)]) Σ^2
*   K = (u'/l) ΓK

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Flow Chart**

    *   Existence of Flamlets (Section 2)
    *   Flame-Vortex Interaction (Section 2)
    *   Stretch Rate K (Section 2)
    *   Residence Time of Eddy in Flame
    *   Flamelet Wrinkling Process (Section 1.1)
    *   Thermo-Diffusive Process (Section 4.3)
    *   Damkohler: ST - Area (Section 3.3)
    *   Theory of Flame Stretch
    *   Flamelet Area/Vol (Section 3.1), Label: Σ
    *   Strength of Flamlets (Section 4.3), Label: I0

*   **Vortex-Flame Interaction**

    *   Arrows showing premixed reactants approaching.
    *   Vortex (circular flow pattern)
    *   Flame (near the vortex)
   *   ST/SL,0=1ο Σ δη
*   FLAME
*   VORTEX

  

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   beta0 = 1.0
*   C = 0.5
*   k = turbulent kinetic energy
*   YF0 = upstream of the brush
*   tres = residence time of interaction
*   Rc = radius of curvature of the interface.
*   K = stretch rate
*   Σ = flamelet area/vol
*   ΓK = stretch efficiency function
*   Q = mean quenching rate
*   M = merging rate
*   YF = mean fuel mass fraction

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 17]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Σ = 4Σmax(1 - 2)
*   2 = [1 + exp(-4(η - ηm) / δτ)]^-1

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*Table 3: Mean stretch rate (K) and the stretch efficiency function ΓK measured in the turbulent Bunsen flame of Filatyev et al. [53]*

| Case | Height x (mm) | u'/SLo | Integral Scale (mm) | Mean stretch rate (s^-1) | Stretch efficiency function ΓK |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 3b  | 21   | 0.69 | 5.2  | 79  | 1.57 |
| 3b  | 56   | 0.69 | 5.2  | 86  | 1.71 |
| 3c  | 13   | 2.14 | 3.1  | 184 | 0.70 |
| 3c | 31   | 2.14 | 3.1 | 174 | 0.66 |
| 8b  | 26   | 1.88 | 12.8 | 223 | 4.00 |
| 8b  | 61   | 1.88 | 12.8 | 248 | 4.44 |
| 8b  | 96   | 1.88 | 12.8 | 229 | 4.10 |
| 8c  | 26   | 4.47 | 8.7  | 389 | 1.99 |
| 8c  | 61   | 4.47 | 8.7  | 232 | 1.19 |

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   Mean stretch rates varied from 79 to 389 s^-1.
*   Instantaneous stretch rates oscillated between +2500 and -1000 s^-1.
*   Stretch rate of 1800 s^-1 is required to extinguish a counterflow flame.
*   Convection velocities of 3-12 m/s.
*   Σmax: Surface density at the center of the brush where 2 is 0.5.
*   δτ: Flame brush thickness.
*   S₁ was nearly equal to SLO.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 13a:** Flame surface density measured in Bunsen flames of Chen and Bilger plotted against mean reactedness.

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 4:** Flame surface density at the center of the brush (Σmax), brush thickness, and local consumption speeds for several experiments
    *   **Headers:** Ref., Geometry, Σmax (mm-1), Brush thickness δT (mm), ST,LC/SL,0

        *   Deschamps et al. [80], Bunsen, 0.30, 20.0, 6.0
        *   Lee et al. [81], Bunsen, 0.60, 13.0, 7.8
        *   Chen and Bilger [66], Bunsen, 0.50, 6.0, 3.0

        *   Shy et al. [85], Cruciform, 0.14, 9.0, 1.3

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Ratio AT/AL = 2.4
*   constant to correct the 2-D approximation = 1.35

*   ηm: Value of η where c equals 0.5

*   δT: Brush thickness
*   AT: Total flame area
*   AL: Laser sheet area
*   ST,LC: Local consumption speed
*   SLO: Reference consumption speed
*   Σmax : Flame surface density at the center of the brush

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 20]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   K-factor = (u'/SLO)^(-1) * [5.4 + 2.3(u'/SLO)] / [1 + 2.3(u'/SLO)]

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 17 (left):**

    *   Axes:
        *   x: x (cm)
        *   y: Surface Density (1/mm)
    *   Curves: present study (represented by solid line with circular data points) and Deschamps et al [25]
*   **Figure 17 (bottom left):**

    *   Axes:
        *   x: K-factor (x10^3 m^-1)
        *   y: Σ_MAX (m^-1)
    *   Curves: Present study (represented by open circles) and Deschamps et al [25] (represented by filled squares).
*   **Figure 17 (right):**

    *   Axes:
        *   x: (x-x0)/sigma
        *   y: Normalized Flame Surface Density
    *   Curves: Data points scattered around a central peak

*   **Figure 18:**

    *   Axes:
        *   x: Turbulence Intensity u'/SLo
        *   y: ST/SL0
    *   Curves:
        *   Turbulent Displacement Speed ST,LD (represented by open diamonds)

title: Turbulent_premixednext_term_combustion_Flamelet_structure_and_its_effect_on_turb
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 26]
**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   dG/dt + v * del(G) = (S_L + S_T,SG) |del(G)| + ((rho_R/rho_P) - 1) S_L0/2 * I(G) (Equation 32)
*   S_L = S_L0 (1 - L * K) (Equation 33a)
*   K = -del * (del(G) / |del(G)|) (Equation 33b)
*   S_T,SG / S_L0 = A * (A * S_L / D) + B * (A * S_L / D)^2 + C * (D_T / D)^(1/2) (Equation 34)
*   rho_R * U_R,N = rho_P * U_P,N

**2. Graphs/charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   S_L: Stretched laminar burning velocity
*   S_L0: Laminar burning velocity (unstretched)
*   S_T,SG: Subgrid contribution to turbulent burning velocity
*   G: A scalar quantity defined near the interface
*   v: Unsteady resolved-scale velocity field
*   L: Markstein length
*   K: Local curvature of the interface
*   Ma_L: Markstein number
*   A, B, C: Constants
*   D: Diffusivity
*   D_T: Turbulent diffusivity
*   rho_R: Reactant density
*   rho_P: Product density
*   U_R,N: Reactant velocity normal to the interface
*   U_P,N: Product Velocity normal to the interface
*   N: indicates the direction normal to the interface
*   I(G): Sivashinsky integral
*   delta_LO: laminar flame thickness
* Esg: surface area

text:

Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
*   SL/SL0 = 1 - MaL * K / (SL0^2/z0^2) (35)
*   SF.C/SL0 = 1 - MaF * K / (SL0^2/z0^2) (36)
*   SF.C/SL0 = I0 = 1 - MaT * u'/l / (SL0^2/z0^2) (37)
*   MaL = 3.3*phi - 2.3 (methane-air, 0.5 < phi < 1.4)
*   MaL = -8.6*phi + 12.0 (propane-air, 0.5 < phi < 1.7) (38)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   ST,C,G/SL0 = 1 + 0.0021 * ((u'/SL0) - 0.16 *(u'/SL0)^2)^(1/2) * (U/SL0) * (l/delta_L0)^1/2 * (W/delta_L0)^1/2 * f(Ma)
*   ST,C,G/SL0 = 1 + A2 * (u'/(SL0*delta_L0))^1/2
*   A2 = 0.00077 * (U/SL0) * (W/delta_L0)^1/2 * f(Ma)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 44**:
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: (u'/S_L0)^1/2 * Re_A^1/4 (no units given for Re_A)
        *   Y-axis: S_T/S_L0 (dimensionless)
    *   Curves: Scatter plot representing turbulent burning velocity of Bunsen flames.
    *   Key Values: Propane-air, φ = 1

*   **Figure 46**:
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Turbulence Intensity u' (m/s)
        *   Y-axis: Turbulent Burning Velocity S_T (cm/s)
    *   Curves: Plots showing the relationship between turbulent burning velocity and turbulence intensity for various fuel/N2/AIR mixtures.
    *   Key Values:  Data sets labeled (H07-15N Le=0.41, P15-15N, M07-15N, H09-15N, M09-15N Le=0.89, P114-15N, M098-15N, P07-15N Le=1.59),  P0 = 0.101 MPa, T0 = 298 K, SL0 = 15 cm/s.  Data points are for CH4(circle), C3H8 (square), H2 (triangle).

*   **Figure 45**:
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: q'/S_Lo (dimensionless)
        *   Y-axis: (S_T,LC/S_L0)/I_0 (dimensionless)
    *   Curves:  Scatter plot of normalized tubulent burning velocity of Buflames
    *   Key Values: Data for fuels of methane and propane, φ = 0.65-1.0.

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   S_L0 = 15 cm/s

*   P0 = 0.101 MPa

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 47:**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   x-axis: Wrinkle Height/Wavelength (H/L)
        *   y-axis: (AT/AL)/(PT/PL)^2
    *   **Units:** dimensionless
    *   **Curves:**
        *   Rectangular Wrinkles (solid line at the top)
        *   Triangular Wrinkles (solid line at the bottom)
        *   Expected Shape of Turbulent Wrinkles (dashed line in the middle)

        *   The y-axis equals to 1 when (Pr/PL) is estimate of 3-D area ratio

**4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   H: height of wrinkle
*   L: wavelength of wrinkle, base size for prism
*   AT: Actual area
*   AL: Smoothed area
*   PT: Actual perimeter
*   PL: Smoothed perimeter
*   D: 3-D fractal dimension
*   TAT: Time required to raise temperature of reactants to ignition temperature
*   Tig: Chemical ignition delay time
*   10 atm: increased gas pressure
*   20%: typical uncertainty of flame perimeter method to estimate 3-D surface area
*   1.0: value added to the measured 2D fractal dimension to estimate the 3D fractal dimension

    *   Ka > (rho_R/rho_P)^(3/2) (Equation 47)

2.  **Graphs/Charts:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3.  **Tables:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4.  **Diagrams:**
    NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5.  **Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**
    *   d : diameter of eddies
    *   dro : diameter of eddies
    *   DT : turbulent diffusivity
    *   D : molecular diffusivity
    *   ue,F : rotational velocity
    *   delta_L0: (unspecified scale, but used as eddy diameter)
    *   l: (unspecified scale, but used to scale eddy diameter)
    *   rho_P: density
    *   rho_R: density
    *   u': (unspecified rotational velocity)
    *   SLO: the laminar flame thickness
    *   Κα: Karlovitz number
    * ug: rotational velocity
    *  u(d/l)^(1/3): scales as d^(-2/3)
    *   δF has been eliminated by setting it equal to D/SLO
    *   The right side of Eq. (47) is typically 17
    *   the additional velocity is 5-7 times the laminar burning velocity
    *   the volume of each eddy expands by a factor of 5-7
    *   the mean vorticity decreases by a factor of 5-7

**2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 9**: Wavelengths (lambda_min) of the smallest wrinkles of premixed turbulent flames, which are 12-25 times the Taylor scale, indicating that eddies smaller than ten Taylor scales do not cause wrinkles.

    | Reference             | Wavelength of smallest wrinkle lambda_min (mm) | Integral scale l (mm) | lambda_min/l | lambda_min/lambda_Taylor | lambda_min/deltaLO |
    | :-------------------- | :------------------------------------------ | :-------------------- | :----------- | :----------------------- | :----------------- |
    | Filatyev et al. [53] | 2                                           | 5.2                   | 0.4          | 20                      | 6.7                |
    | Cheng et al. [58]    | 2                                           | 10.4                  | 0.2          | 24                      | 4.0                |
    | Lachaux et al. [59]   | 4                                           | 3.0                   | 1.3          | 25                      | 3.1                |
    | Lachaux et al. [59]   | 2                                           | 3.1                   | 0.6          | 12                      | 3.1                |
    | Shepherd et al. [60]  | 1.5                                         | 3.0                   | 0.5          | 13                      | 5.0                |
    | Buschmann et al. [61] | 2                                           | 12                    | 0.2          | 22                      | 5.0                |

**4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT ("Fig. 48. It is a schematic showing two counter-rotating eddies on the left that exert a positive stretch rate, while the two eddies on the right are rotating in the opposite direction.")

*   240 cm/s - Velocity of products due to flame perturbing
*   C1 - constant
*   C2 - constant proportional to (U/SLO)^2

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Components:**
    *   Products
    *   Reactants

*   **Labels:**
    *   positive stretch creates negative curvature
    *   negative stretch creates positive curvature

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Differences between predictions and measurements were 4% and 19%, respectively.
*   Karlovitz number greatly exceeds unity.
*   Preheat zone average thickness is 2-4 times larger than the laminar value in some cases.
*   Markstein number (thermodiffusive effects)
*   DNS results of Chakraborty and Cant [89] have quantified this negative correlation.
*   DNS results of Bell et al. [1,2] have demonstrated that it is now possible to predict reasonable values of the turbulent burning velocity, using no modeling constants, of the Bunsen experiment of Filatyev et al. [53] and the V-flame of Cheng and Shepherd [2] for realistic Reynolds numbers.
